message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Next: rbrr-depot-identity-collapse (₢BBAAM) [rough]

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Foundational simplification before BBAAK. Collapses depot identity in RBRR
  to (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER) — project_id, GAR repository, pool stem, and
  related derived names fall out of those two at kindle. Drops the timestamp
  from project_id; CLOUD_PREFIX flows uniformly through derived names,
  displacing today's hard-coded `rbw-`. Levy fails if derived resources
  already exist; the soft-delete window becomes operator-visible rather than
  hidden behind a timestamp.
  
  Mechanical at call sites, conceptually load-bearing. Docket holds intent
  only — implementation specifics surface at commit time.
  
  ## Scope
  
  - Reshape `rbgp_depot_levy`: project_id and sub-resource names derive from
    (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER); no timestamp.
  - Collapse RBRR fields: RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY and RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM removed,
    replaced by RBDC-derived constants computed at kindle.
  - Reshape `rbgp_depot_list`: emit one fact file per known depot in
    BURD_OUTPUT_DIR (basename keyed on moniker; content = state value).
    State vocabulary in BCG-compliant tinder/kindle constants.
  - Retrofit pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 to autodetect next free moniker
    via depot-list (theurge-side — autodetect is NOT in payor code).
    Six-digit numeric increment starting at the 100000 floor per family
    prefix (e.g., pristq100000, pristg100000).
  - Marshal-zero blanking adjusted to match the new RBRR field set.
  - RBS0 and operator banner updated to match.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
  - BURD_OUTPUT_DIR wipe at every tabtarget entry (already enforced by
    bud_dispatch.sh) is the lifecycle anchor: fact files and other artifacts
    are ephemeral; fixturing must read every needed output before invoking
    the next tabtarget.
  - Real-time review at each step before commit. No pre-engineered choices
    beyond intent.
  - BCG compliance for new state-value vocabulary (tinder/kindle constants).
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - BBAAK cases 4-5 (smaller follow-on pace, rides on this).
  - Operator-handbook restructuring beyond removing the post-levy
    banner-copy instruction.
  - Bucket / Mason SA naming uniformity — design call at implementation, not
    a pre-locked redesign.
  - 409-fatal posture for currently-idempotent sub-resources — design call
    at implementation.

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Design decisions
10	
11	### Single operator prerequisite chain
12	
13	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
14	
15	### Entry contract — load-bearing
16	
17	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
18	- Working tree clean
19	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
20	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
21	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
22	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
23	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
24	
25	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
26	
27	### Pristine-lifecycle fixture architecture
28	
29	The marshal-zero gate AND the SA/depot lifecycle exercises live as cases inside one new theurge fixture (`pristine-lifecycle`):
30	
31	| # | Case | Idempotent? | Cost |
32	|---|------|-------------|------|
33	| 1 | `marshal-zero-attestation` | N/A — gate | Free |
34	| 2 | `depot-lifecycle` | Yes (throwaway-named) | One GCP project per run (soft-delete graveyard accepted) |
35	| 3 | `governor-lifecycle` | Yes (throwaway-named) | One GCP project per run (case levies its own throwaway depot to satisfy `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` precondition; depot is unmade in case cleanup) |
36	| 4 | `retriever-lifecycle` | Yes (throwaway governor + retriever) | Free + light read probe |
37	| 5 | `director-lifecycle` | Yes (throwaway governor + director) | Light Cloud Build / GAR write probe |
38	
39	Cases 4 and 5 each create their own throwaway governor (single-case independence is load-bearing); after the case, both throwaway SAs are forfeited. Cases 2-5 are idempotent against existing canonical state — they can run while a canonical depot/SA chain exists, against any state.
40	
41	**Single-case run** (e.g., `tt/rbtd-s.SingleCase.pristine-lifecycle.sh depot-lifecycle`) skips case 1's gate and exercises just the named case. **Full-fixture run** goes through case 1 first; case 1 gates the rest.
42	
43	### Canonical infrastructure setup is a separate phase
44	
45	After the pristine-lifecycle fixture passes, `rbw-tP` proceeds to canonical infrastructure setup (BBAAC): mantle real governor + deploy real RBRA, charter real retriever + deploy RBRA, knight real director + deploy RBRA, levy canonical depot. This persistent state is what reliquary inscribe, hallmark builds, and crucible runs depend on.
46	
47	Two depots created per pristine run: one throwaway (case 2 of fixture) + one canonical (BBAAC). Both quota-bearing. Case 3 also levies+unmakes its own throwaway depot — counted as a third quota-bearing event but cleaned up within the case.
48	
49	### Failure mode contract
50	
51	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
52	
53	### Release-branch execution contract
54	
55	Three properties bind every case in this fixture. They shape how a run is shaped — distinct from, and constraining, the failure-mode contract above.
56	
57	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
58	
59	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each fixture step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that. Minimizing fixture commits is anti-goal.
60	
61	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A case fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches, no "cleanup so the next case can run." Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. The operator never asks "should I continue past this failure?" — the answer is always no.
62	
63	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance for graceful continuation, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy tee files — is non-load-bearing complexity and does not belong in pristine-lifecycle code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
64	
65	### Implementation patterns (locked during BBAAJ)
66	
67	Durable choices binding all pristine-lifecycle case implementations (BBAAB, BBAAJ, BBAAK, and future BBAAC vessel-commit cases).
68	
69	**Throwaway-prefix mechanism: edit `.rbk/rbrr.env` + git commit.** Cases 2-5 need non-blank `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX`/`RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX` to invoke depot/SA tabtargets. The fixture mimics the human workflow: open rbrr.env, edit, `git add` + `git commit`. After the fixture runs, HEAD has walked off marshal-zero — recovery is `rbw-MZ`, matching the "start-over-from-zero" failure mode above.
70	
71	Rejected alternative — env-var override: `rbrr_cli.sh` kindle does `source rbrr_regime.sh; source ${RBBC_rbrr_file}; zrbrr_kindle; zrbrr_enforce` — sourcing rbrr.env overwrites any pre-set env, then enforce locks `readonly`. Pre-set env is silently discarded. Confirmed by tracing.
72	
73	Rejected alternative — in-memory restore (RAII): mutates rbrr.env without committing, leaves a transient dirty-tree window where a crash or sibling tooling sees uncommitted state that isn't operator-authored. Edit-and-commit is honest.
74	
75	**Idempotent install helper.** A shared function `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes` reads current rbrr.env values; if blank (post-marshal-zero), writes throwaway values and commits; if already throwaway, no-op. Cases 2-5 call this as their first step. One commit per fixture run, not per case.
76	
77	**Depot precondition for governor-mantle (case 3).** `rbgp_governor_mantle` requires `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` set — post-marshal-zero it is blank. Case 3 levies its own throwaway depot (named distinctly from case 2's, e.g. `pristg01` vs `pristq01`), edits rbrr.env to set `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` and commits, runs the governor lifecycle, then unmakes the depot in cleanup. The same pattern (commit a small targeted edit, run, clean up) is the model for future cases that need additional rbrr/rbrv/rbrn fields populated.
78	
79	**Tabtarget invocation style — fact files over stdout parsing.** Tabtargets that emit identifying values (depot project_ids, SA emails) write `rbgp_fact_*` files via `buf_write_fact`; cases consume via `rbtdri_invoke_global` + `rbtdri_read_burv_fact`. The BURV-isolated invoke also makes invoke counters and per-call output capture available to the case. Handbook-style tabtargets without facts (e.g., `rbtdrf_handbook.rs` cases) keep the raw `Command::new(&tt).current_dir(&root)` style. For imprint-channel tabtargets like `rbtd-ap` (cases 4-5), use `rbtdri_find_tabtarget` with the role as imprint.
80	
81	**Section structure inside `RBTDRP_SECTIONS_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE`.**
82	- §1 `pristine-lifecycle-gate` — case 1 only (BBAAB-landed)
83	- §2 `pristine-lifecycle-simple` — cases 2-3 (BBAAJ)
84	- §3 `pristine-lifecycle-credentialed` — cases 4-5 (BBAAK)
85	
86	`rbtdrc_fixture_fail_fast` is true for pristine-lifecycle, so §1 failure short-circuits §2 and §3 in full-fixture mode. Single-case mode skips the gate.
87	
88	**Vessel commits beyond BBAAJ/BBAAK.** As pristine-tier extends to BBAAC and beyond (reliquary inscribe / enshrine / ordain / kludge), additional commits in vessel `rbrv.env` files and similar regime artifacts use the same edit-and-commit shape. The qualification's commit trail is the operator's audit trail.
89	
90	**Git shell-out style.** Mirror jjx/vvx — `Command::new("git").args([...]).current_dir(&root).output()`. Stage with explicit file path (never `git add -A`); commit messages produced via Rust string formatting. No `--no-verify`, no `--amend`.
91	
92	### Tier layering
93	
94	Three tiers, escalating cost:
95	
96	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
97	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
98	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
99	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
100	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
101	
102	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
103	
104	### Operator scope
105	
106	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section (5 human steps).
107	
108	### Coupling to ₣A_
109	
110	This heat runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces. ₣A_'s AAE+AAF+AAG sequence is the one-time cutover that informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence; pristine-tier construction proceeds in parallel and codifies the lessons. BBAAG's first end-to-end run is the natural validation point for both heats together.
111	
112	## References
113	
114	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap; AAE+AAF+AAG one-time burn-in informs but doesn't codify
115	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — RBLM Lifecycle Marshal; `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime, deletes all RBRAs, blanks hallmark/depot-scoped vessel fields. Marshal-zero signature is baked here per BBAAB.
116	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
117	- `Tools/rbk/rbh0/rbhocd_credential_director.sh` / `rbhocr_credential_retriever.sh` — handbook tracks describing director "build access" and retriever "pull/tally access" — operations the lifecycle cases 4 and 5 must verify
118	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)
119	- Recent spook commits informing the bug class: kludge-aware-charge-prereq (₢BAAAH), ZRBOB_PROJECT extraction (f7146d1d), rbob_charged_predicate fix (a8eb7311), reliquary integrity-broken negative-test residue (recent ₣BA verification)
120	- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrf_handbook.rs` — non-context tabtarget shell-out reference pattern for handbook-style cases (no fact files)
121	- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdri_invocation.rs` — fact-file invocation pattern: `rbtdri_invoke_global` + `rbtdri_read_burv_fact` for cases that need to capture identifying values
122	- `Tools/rbk/rbrr_cli.sh` — kindle architecture documented in Implementation patterns; sourcing-over-env behavior locked the throwaway mechanism choice
123	
124	# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAM
125	
126	## Character
127	
128	Foundational simplification before BBAAK. Collapses depot identity in RBRR
129	to (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER) — project_id, GAR repository, pool stem, and
130	related derived names fall out of those two at kindle. Drops the timestamp
131	from project_id; CLOUD_PREFIX flows uniformly through derived names,
132	displacing today's hard-coded `rbw-`. Levy fails if derived resources
133	already exist; the soft-delete window becomes operator-visible rather than
134	hidden behind a timestamp.
135	
136	Mechanical at call sites, conceptually load-bearing. Docket holds intent
137	only — implementation specifics surface at commit time.
138	
139	## Scope
140	
141	- Reshape `rbgp_depot_levy`: project_id and sub-resource names derive from
142	  (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER); no timestamp.
143	- Collapse RBRR fields: RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY and RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM removed,
144	  replaced by RBDC-derived constants computed at kindle.
145	- Reshape `rbgp_depot_list`: emit one fact file per known depot in
146	  BURD_OUTPUT_DIR (basename keyed on moniker; content = state value).
147	  State vocabulary in BCG-compliant tinder/kindle constants.
148	- Retrofit pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 to autodetect next free moniker
149	  via depot-list (theurge-side — autodetect is NOT in payor code).
150	  Six-digit numeric increment starting at the 100000 floor per family
151	  prefix (e.g., pristq100000, pristg100000).
152	- Marshal-zero blanking adjusted to match the new RBRR field set.
153	- RBS0 and operator banner updated to match.
154	
155	## Constraints
156	
157	- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
158	- BURD_OUTPUT_DIR wipe at every tabtarget entry (already enforced by
159	  bud_dispatch.sh) is the lifecycle anchor: fact files and other artifacts
160	  are ephemeral; fixturing must read every needed output before invoking
161	  the next tabtarget.
162	- Real-time review at each step before commit. No pre-engineered choices
163	  beyond intent.
164	- BCG compliance for new state-value vocabulary (tinder/kindle constants).
165	
166	## Out of scope
167	
168	- BBAAK cases 4-5 (smaller follow-on pace, rides on this).
169	- Operator-handbook restructuring beyond removing the post-levy
170	  banner-copy instruction.
171	- Bucket / Mason SA naming uniformity — design call at implementation, not
172	  a pre-locked redesign.
173	- 409-fatal posture for currently-idempotent sub-resources — design call
174	  at implementation.
175

| # | Case | Idempotent? | Cost |

| 1 | `marshal-zero-attestation` | N/A — gate | Free |

| 3 | `governor-lifecycle` | Yes (throwaway-named) | One GCP project per run (case levies its own throwaway depot to satisfy `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` precondition; depot is unmade in case cleanup) |

**Single-case run** (e.g., `tt/rbtd-s.SingleCase.pristine-lifecycle.sh depot-lifecycle`) skips case 1's gate and exercises just the named case. **Full-fixture run** goes through case 1 first; case 1 gates the rest.

After the pristine-lifecycle fixture passes, `rbw-tP` proceeds to canonical infrastructure setup (BBAAC): mantle real governor + deploy real RBRA, charter real retriever + deploy RBRA, knight real director + deploy RBRA, levy canonical depot. Two depots created per pristine run: one throwaway (case 2 of fixture) + one canonical (BBAAC). Both quota-bearing. Case 3 also levies+unmakes its own throwaway depot — counted as a third quota-bearing event but cleaned up within the case.

Three properties bind every case in this fixture. They shape how a run is shaped — distinct from, and constraining, the failure-mode contract above.

The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

**Throwaway-prefix mechanism: edit `.rbk/rbrr.env` + git commit.** Cases 2-5 need non-blank `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX`/`RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX` to invoke depot/SA tabtargets. **Depot precondition for governor-mantle (case 3).** `rbgp_governor_mantle` requires `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` set — post-marshal-zero it is blank. **Section structure inside `RBTDRP_SECTIONS_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE`.**

- §2 `pristine-lifecycle-simple` — cases 2-3 (BBAAJ)
- §3 `pristine-lifecycle-credentialed` — cases 4-5 (BBAAK)

`rbtdrc_fixture_fail_fast` is true for pristine-lifecycle, so §1 failure short-circuits §2 and §3 in full-fixture mode. Single-case mode skips the gate.

**Vessel commits beyond BBAAJ/BBAAK.** As pristine-tier extends to BBAAC and beyond (reliquary inscribe / enshrine / ordain / kludge), additional commits in vessel `rbrv.env` files and similar regime artifacts use the same edit-and-commit shape. The qualification's commit trail is the operator's audit trail.

**Git shell-out style.** Mirror jjx/vvx — `Command::new("git").args([...]).current_dir(&root).output()`. Stage with explicit file path (never `git add -A`); commit messages produced via Rust string formatting. No `--no-verify`, no `--amend`.

### Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

### Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section (5 human steps).

### Coupling to ₣A_

This heat runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces. ₣A_'s AAE+AAF+AAG sequence is the one-time cutover that informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence; pristine-tier construction proceeds in parallel and codifies the lessons. BBAAG's first end-to-end run is the natural validation point for both heats together.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap; AAE+AAF+AAG one-time burn-in informs but doesn't codify
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — RBLM Lifecycle Marshal; `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime, deletes all RBRAs, blanks hallmark/depot-scoped vessel fields. Marshal-zero signature is baked here per BBAAB.
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rbh0/rbhocd_credential_director.sh` / `rbhocr_credential_retriever.sh` — handbook tracks describing director "build access" and retriever "pull/tally access" — operations the lifecycle cases 4 and 5 must verify
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)
- Recent spook commits informing the bug class: kludge-aware-charge-prereq (₢BAAAH), ZRBOB_PROJECT extraction (f7146d1d), rbob_charged_predicate fix (a8eb7311), reliquary integrity-broken negative-test residue (recent ₣BA verification)
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrf_handbook.rs` — non-context tabtarget shell-out reference pattern for handbook-style cases (no fact files)
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdri_invocation.rs` — fact-file invocation pattern: `rbtdri_invoke_global` + `rbtdri_read_burv_fact` for cases that need to capture identifying values
- `Tools/rbk/rbrr_cli.sh` — kindle architecture documented in Implementation patterns; sourcing-over-env behavior locked the throwaway mechanism choice

# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAM

## Character

Foundational simplification before BBAAK. Collapses depot identity in RBRR
to (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER) — project_id, GAR repository, pool stem, and
related derived names fall out of those two at kindle. Drops the timestamp
from project_id; CLOUD_PREFIX flows uniformly through derived names,
displacing today's hard-coded `rbw-`. Levy fails if derived resources
already exist; the soft-delete window becomes operator-visible rather than
hidden behind a timestamp.

Mechanical at call sites, conceptually load-bearing. Docket holds intent
only — implementation specifics surface at commit time.

## Scope

- Reshape `rbgp_depot_levy`: project_id and sub-resource names derive from
  (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER); no timestamp.
- Collapse RBRR fields: RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY and RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM removed,
  replaced by RBDC-derived constants computed at kindle.
- Reshape `rbgp_depot_list`: emit one fact file per known depot in
  BURD_OUTPUT_DIR (basename keyed on moniker; content = state value).
  State vocabulary in BCG-compliant tinder/kindle constants.
- Retrofit pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 to autodetect next free moniker
  via depot-list (theurge-side — autodetect is NOT in payor code).
  Six-digit numeric increment starting at the 100000 floor per family
  prefix (e.g., pristq100000, pristg100000).
- Marshal-zero blanking adjusted to match the new RBRR field set.
- RBS0 and operator banner updated to match.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- BURD_OUTPUT_DIR wipe at every tabtarget entry (already enforced by
  bud_dispatch.sh) is the lifecycle anchor: fact files and other artifacts
  are ephemeral; fixturing must read every needed output before invoking
  the next tabtarget.
- Real-time review at each step before commit. No pre-engineered choices
  beyond intent.
- BCG compliance for new state-value vocabulary (tinder/kindle constants).

## Out of scope

- BBAAK cases 4-5 (smaller follow-on pace, rides on this).
- Operator-handbook restructuring beyond removing the post-levy
  banner-copy instruction.
- Bucket / Mason SA naming uniformity — design call at implementation, not
  a pre-locked redesign.
- 409-fatal posture for currently-idempotent sub-resources — design call
  at implementation.

content.text:
## Heat ₣BB — rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification *(racing, 4/12)*

**Paddock summary.** Builds `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third release-qualification tier that refuses to run unless marshal-zero state was just committed. Catches the silent first-build bug class via a five-case pristine-lifecycle theurge fixture (gate → depot → governor → retriever → director). Release-branch execution contract is now load-bearing: run on a release branch, commits during the run are first-class (operator's mental anchor against container-recipe cognitive drift), stop on very first failure with no graceful degradation. Implementation patterns from BBAAJ are locked: edit-and-commit `rbrr.env` (env-override blocked at kindle, RAII rejected as dishonest), shared `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes` helper, fact-file invocation via `rbtdri_invoke_global` + `rbtdri_read_burv_fact`.

**Recent work.** BBAAJ landed cases 2-3 with fact-file pattern. BBAAK Step 1 attempted post-levy field extension, then was reverted (additive `git revert --no-commit + jjx_record`) — in-conversation review surfaced that the pre-engineered docket compounded earlier scope expansion. Pace was rescoped to docket/paddock cleanup. Latest commit added the Release-branch execution contract to the paddock.

**Next pace: rbrr-depot-identity-collapse (₢BBAAM) [rough]**

Foundational simplification before BBAAK. Collapse depot identity in RBRR to **(CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER)** — project_id, GAR repository, pool stem, and related derived names fall out at kindle. Drops the timestamp from project_id; CLOUD_PREFIX displaces today's hard-coded `rbw-` uniformly. Levy fails if derived resources already exist (soft-delete window becomes operator-visible). `rbgp_depot_list` emits one fact file per known depot in `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`. Pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 retrofit to autodetect next free moniker via depot-list (theurge-side, six-digit increment from 100000 floor per family prefix). Marshal-zero blanking, RBS0, operator banner updated to match.

**Character:** "Mechanical at call sites, conceptually load-bearing. Docket holds intent only — implementation specifics surface at commit time."

### Name assessment

`rbrr-depot-identity-collapse` fits the docket well — "depot identity" is exactly what reduces (project_id + gar_repository + gcb_pool_stem → derived from CLOUD_PREFIX + MONIKER), and "collapse" captures the dimensional reduction. I'd keep it.

### Proposed approach

- **Real-time review pace, not a delegate-and-wait pace.** The docket explicitly says "No pre-engineered choices beyond intent" and "Real-time review at each step before commit" — the previous BBAAK reversion is fresh evidence that pre-staging the order/shapes goes badly here.
- **Likely natural ordering** (subject to discussion at each commit): kindle-side derived constants in RBDC → payor levy reshape (drop timestamp, derive sub-resource names from CLOUD_PREFIX + MONIKER) → marshal-zero blanking adjusted to match new field set → `rbgp_depot_list` fact-file emission with BCG tinder/kindle state vocabulary → theurge autodetect-next-free-moniker retrofit for cases 2-3 → RBS0 + operator banner updates.
- **Execution:** sequential opus — touches kindle architecture, RBRR contract, payor levy, derived constants, theurge fixture, spec, banner copy. Multi-file but each step is small.

### Strategy

Sequential opus, dialogue-driven. Each step: I propose the change in conversation, you review intent, I implement and notch. No pre-engineered patches.

Ready to proceed? If so, I'd start by reading `rbgp_Payor.sh` (current `rbgp_depot_levy`), `rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh`, `rbrr_regime.sh`, and `rblm_cli.sh` to confirm the surface area before proposing the first step.

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Derived Constants - Credential file path resolution

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBDC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbdc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBDC_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbdc_*)

zrbdc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBDC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbdc already kindled"
  zrbrr_sentinel

  # Ensure secrets directory and role subdirectories exist
  mkdir -p "${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_governor}" \
           "${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_retriever}" \
           "${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_director}" \
           "${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_payor}" \
           "${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_assay}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to create secrets directories under: ${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}"

  # One-shot migration: move old flat rbra-{role}.env to {role}/rbra.env
  local z_mig_role=""
  for z_mig_role in "${RBCC_role_governor}" "${RBCC_role_retriever}" "${RBCC_role_director}"; do
    local z_mig_old="${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/rbra-${z_mig_role}.env"
    local z_mig_new="${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${z_mig_role}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"
    if test -f "${z_mig_old}" && test -f "${z_mig_new}"; then
      buc_die "Migration ambiguous for role ${z_mig_role}: both ${z_mig_old} and ${z_mig_new} exist; delete the obsolete file before continuing"
    fi
    if test -f "${z_mig_old}" && ! test -f "${z_mig_new}"; then
      mv "${z_mig_old}" "${z_mig_new}" || buc_die "Failed to migrate: ${z_mig_old} → ${z_mig_new}"
      local z_has_role=0
      local z_mig_line
      while IFS= read -r z_mig_line; do
        case "${z_mig_line}" in RBRA_ROLE=*) z_has_role=1; break ;; esac
      done < "${z_mig_new}"
      if test "${z_has_role}" = "0"; then
        printf 'RBRA_ROLE=%s\n' "${z_mig_role}" >> "${z_mig_new}"
      fi
    fi
  done

  # Payor migration: rbro.env -> payor/rbro.env
  local z_mig_payor_old="${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_rbro_file}"
  local z_mig_payor_new="${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_payor}/${RBCC_rbro_file}"
  if test -f "${z_mig_payor_old}" && test -f "${z_mig_payor_new}"; then
    buc_die "Migration ambiguous: both ${z_mig_payor_old} and ${z_mig_payor_new} exist; delete the obsolete file before continuing"
  fi
  if test -f "${z_mig_payor_old}"; then
    mv "${z_mig_payor_old}" "${z_mig_payor_new}" || buc_die "Failed to migrate: ${z_mig_payor_old} → ${z_mig_payor_new}"
  fi

  # Derive credential file paths from RBRR_SECRETS_DIR
  readonly RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE="${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_governor}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"
  readonly RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE="${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_retriever}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"
  readonly RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE="${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"
  readonly RBDC_PAYOR_RBRO_FILE="${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_payor}/${RBCC_rbro_file}"
  readonly RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE="${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_assay}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"

  # Derive full pool resource paths from stem (suffixes match RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_TETHER/AIRGAP)
  readonly RBDC_POOL_TETHER="projects/${RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBRR_GCP_REGION}/workerPools/${RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM}-tether"
  readonly RBDC_POOL_AIRGAP="projects/${RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBRR_GCP_REGION}/workerPools/${RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM}-airgap"

  readonly ZRBDC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbdc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBDC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbdc not kindled - call zrbdc_kindle first"
}

# eof

rbrr_validate() {
  buc_doc_brief "Validate RBRR repo regime configuration via enrollment report"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Validating RBRR repo regime file: ${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  buv_report RBRR "Repository Regime"
  buc_step "RBRR repo regime valid"
}

# Command: render - diagnostic display of all RBRR fields
rbrr_render() {
  buc_doc_brief "Display diagnostic view of RBRR repo regime configuration"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buv_render RBRR "RBRR - Recipe Bottle Regime Repo"
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and Main

zrbrr_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"

  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
  source "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bupr_PresentationRegime.sh"

  zbuv_kindle
  zburd_kindle
  zburd_enforce
  zrbcc_kindle

  zrbrr_kindle
  zrbrr_enforce
  zrbdc_kindle

  zbupr_kindle
}

buc_execute rbrr_ "Recipe Bottle Repository Regime" zrbrr_furnish "$@"

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Regime Repo - Validator Module

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBRR_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrr multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBRR_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbrr_*)

zrbrr_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBRR_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrr already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  # Enroll all RBRR variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll RBRR

  buv_group_enroll "Resource Prefixing"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX         2   11  "Prefix prepended to cloud-visible resource names (GAR, Cloud Build, GCP)"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX       2   11  "Prefix prepended to local container and network names"

  buv_group_enroll "Vessel and Local Configuration"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRR_VESSEL_DIR           1  255  "Vessel definitions directory"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE     1   64  "Container workspace path for bottle working directory"
  buv_ipv4_enroll    RBRR_DNS_SERVER                   "DNS server for containers"

  buv_group_enroll "GCP Infrastructure"
  buv_gname_enroll   RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID         6   63  "GCP project ID for depot"
  buv_gname_enroll   RBRR_GCP_REGION               1   32  "GCP region"
  buv_gname_enroll   RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY           1   63  "Google Artifact Registry repository name"

  buv_group_enroll "Google Cloud Build Configuration"
  buv_gname_enroll   RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE           3   64  "Machine type for Cloud Build (CE format)"
  buv_gname_enroll   RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM                1   63  "Worker pool base name (suffixed with -tether/-airgap)"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT                2   10  "Build timeout (e.g., 1200s)"
  buv_decimal_enroll RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS  1  999  "Min concurrent builds required"

  buv_group_enroll "Secrets Directory"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRR_SECRETS_DIR              1  512  "Directory containing credential files"

  buv_group_enroll "Public Docs"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL          1  512  "Public docs URL — readme target for buh_tlt glossary links, updated per-release or per-incorporation"

  # Guard against unexpected RBRR_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel RBRR RBRR_

  # Build docker env args array from validated values
  # Usage: docker run "${ZRBRR_DOCKER_ENV[@]}" ...
  ZRBRR_DOCKER_ENV=("-e" "RBRR_DNS_SERVER=${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}")
  readonly ZRBRR_DOCKER_ENV

  # Lock all enrolled RBRR_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock RBRR

  readonly ZRBRR_KINDLED=1
}

zrbrr_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBRR_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbrr not kindled - call zrbrr_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all RBRR enrollment validations and custom format checks
zrbrr_enforce() {
  zrbrr_sentinel

  buv_vet RBRR

  buv_dir_exists "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}"
  buv_dir_exists "${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}"

  [[ "${RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT}" =~ ^[0-9]+s$ ]] \
    || buc_die "Invalid RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT format: ${RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT} (expected NNNs)"

  [[ "${RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM}" =~ ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]+$ ]] \
    || buc_die "Invalid RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM format: ${RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM} (expected lowercase pool base name)"

  [[ "${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}"   =~ ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*-$ ]] \
    || buc_die "Invalid RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX format: ${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}   (expected lowercase starting with letter, ending in hyphen)"

  [[ "${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX}" =~ ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*-$ ]] \
    || buc_die "Invalid RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX format: ${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX} (expected lowercase starting with letter, ending in hyphen)"

}

# eof

16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle GCP Constants - Implementation (no printf required)
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion detection
24	# (Module state remains ZRBGC_* per BCG; external constants use RBGC_*)
25	test -z "${ZRBGC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
26	ZRBGC_SOURCED=1
27	
28	######################################################################
29	# Internal Functions (zrbgc_*)
30	
31	zrbgc_kindle() {
32	  test -z "${ZRBGC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc already kindled"
33	
34	  # Global Resource Naming (Google Cloud global namespace)
35	  # These resources compete in globally-unique namespaces across all of GCP
36	  # Pattern: {prefix}-{type}-{name}-{timestamp} where timestamp is YYMMDDHHMMSS
37	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX="rbwg"
38	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR="p"
39	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT="d"
40	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_BUCKET="b"
41	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_FORMAT="+%y%m%d%H%M%S"
42	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN=12
43	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX="[0-9]{${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN}}"
44	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_NAME_MAX=10
45	
46	  # Global resource validation patterns
47	  # Payor:  rbwg-p-YYMMDDHHMMSS
48	  # Depot:  rbwg-d-[name]-YYMMDDHHMMSS
49	  # Bucket: rbwg-b-[name]-YYMMDDHHMMSS
50	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PAYOR_REGEX="^${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX}$"
51	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_REGEX="^${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-[a-z0-9-]+-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX}$"
52	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_BUCKET_REGEX="^${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_BUCKET}-[a-z0-9-]+-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX}$"
53	
54	  # Basic Configuration
55	  readonly RBGC_ADMIN_ROLE="rbw-admin"
56	  readonly RBGC_PAYOR_ROLE="rbw-payor"
57	  readonly RBGC_PAYOR_APP_NAME="Recipe Bottle Payor"
58	
59	  # Service Account Prefixes
60	  readonly RBGC_MASON_PREFIX="mason"
61	
62	  # Timeouts
63	  readonly RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC=90
64	  readonly RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC=3
65	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX=7
66	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC=10
67	
68	  # URL Roots & Well-known Endpoints
69	  readonly RBGC_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL="https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token"
70	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM="https://iam.googleapis.com"
71	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM="https://cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com"
72	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_SERVICEUSAGE="https://serviceusage.googleapis.com"
73	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY="https://artifactregistry.googleapis.com"
74	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD="https://cloudbuild.googleapis.com"
75	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING="https://cloudbilling.googleapis.com"
76	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE="https://storage.googleapis.com"
77	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_SECRETMANAGER="https://secretmanager.googleapis.com"
78	  readonly RBGC_CONSOLE_URL="https://console.cloud.google.com/"
79	  readonly RBGC_SIGNUP_URL="https://cloud.google.com/free"
80	
81	  # OAuth Scopes
82	  readonly RBGC_SCOPE_CLOUD_PLATFORM="https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform"
83	
84	  # Service Usage Service Identifiers
85	  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_IAM="iam.googleapis.com"
86	  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_CRM="cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com"
87	  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY="artifactregistry.googleapis.com"
88	
89	  # Email/Domain Assembly
90	  readonly RBGC_SA_EMAIL_DOMAIN="iam.gserviceaccount.com"
91	
92	  # API Version Paths
93	  readonly RBGC_IAM_V1="/v1"
94	  readonly RBGC_CRM_V1="/v1"
95	  readonly RBGC_CRM_V3="/v3"
96	  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1="/v1"
97	  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1BETA1="/v1beta1"
98	  readonly RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1="/v1"
99	  readonly RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1="/v1"
100	  readonly RBGC_CLOUDBILLING_V1="/v1"
101	  readonly RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1="/storage/v1"
102	  readonly RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_UPLOAD="/upload/storage/v1"
103	  readonly RBGC_SECRETMANAGER_V1="/v1"
104	
105	  # REST Path Fragments
106	  readonly RBGC_PATH_PROJECTS="/projects"
107	  readonly RBGC_PATH_LOCATIONS="/locations"
108	  readonly RBGC_PATH_REPOSITORIES="/repositories"
109	  readonly RBGC_PATH_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS="/serviceAccounts"
110	  readonly RBGC_PATH_KEYS="/keys"
111	
112	  # REST Operation Suffixes
113	  readonly RBGC_CRM_GET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX=":getIamPolicy"
114	  readonly RBGC_CRM_SET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX=":setIamPolicy"
115	  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_ENABLE_SUFFIX=":enable"
116	  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_PATH_SERVICES="/services"
117	
118	  # Operation Prefixes
119	  readonly RBGC_OP_PREFIX_GLOBAL="operations/"
120	
121	  # Ark Artifact Basenames (₢A_AAK layout)
122	  # Each ark type is a plain basename sibling under <prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/.
123	  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE="image"
124	  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT="about"
125	  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH="vouch"
126	  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_DIAGS="diags"
127	  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ATTEST="attest"
128	  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_POUCH="pouch"
129	
130	  # GAR Categorical Namespaces (₢A_AAK layout)
131	  # Top-level prefix-rooted namespaces under which hallmark/reliquary/enshrine
132	  # arks are stored as plain basename siblings. Consumed by rbgl_GarLayout.sh.
133	  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS="hallmarks"
134	  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES="reliquaries"
135	  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_ENSHRINES="enshrines"
136	
137	  # Reliquary Tool Basenames (₢A_AAK layout)
138	  # Canonical tool names under <prefix>reliquaries/<date>/. Authoritative
139	  # manifest lives in rbgji/rbgji01-inscribe-mirror.sh.
140	  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD="gcloud"
141	  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER="docker"
142	  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE="alpine"
143	  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT="syft"
144	  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT="binfmt"
145	  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO="skopeo"
146	
147	  # Fact-file filenames (written to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR by producers, read by tests)
148	  readonly RBF_FACT_HALLMARK="rbf_fact_hallmark"
149	  readonly RBF_FACT_BUILD_ID="rbf_fact_build_id"
150	  readonly RBF_FACT_GAR_ROOT="rbf_fact_gar_root"
151	  readonly RBF_FACT_ARK_STEM="rbf_fact_ark_stem"
152	  readonly RBF_FACT_ARK_YIELD="rbf_fact_ark_yield"
153	  readonly RBF_FACT_RELIQUARY="rbf_fact_reliquary"
154	
155	  # Payor fact-file filenames (depot/governor identifying values)
156	  readonly RBGP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID="rbgp_fact_depot_project_id"
157	  readonly RBGP_FACT_GOVERNOR_SA_EMAIL="rbgp_fact_governor_sa_email"
158	
159	  # Artifact Registry (GAR) Composition
160	  readonly RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX="-docker.pkg.dev"
161	
162	
163	  # Canonical Role IDs
164	  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_READER="roles/artifactregistry.reader"
165	  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_WRITER="roles/artifactregistry.writer"
166	  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_ADMIN="roles/artifactregistry.admin"
167	  readonly RBGC_ROLE_CONTAINERANALYSIS_OCCURRENCES_VIEWER="roles/containeranalysis.occurrences.viewer"
168	  readonly RBGC_ROLE_CLOUDBUILD_BUILDS_EDITOR="roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor"
169	
170	  # Common API Base Paths (project-independent)
171	  readonly RBGC_API_BASE_GCS="${RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE}${RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1}"
172	
173	  # Cloud Resource Manager - Liens API
174	  readonly RBGC_API_CRM_LIST_LIENS="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens"
175	  readonly RBGC_API_CRM_DELETE_LIEN="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens"
176	
177	  # Google Cloud Storage (GCS) APIs (project-independent)
178	  readonly RBGC_API_GCS_BUCKETS="${RBGC_API_BASE_GCS}/b"
179	
180	  readonly RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM="linux/amd64"
181	
182	
183	  # Worker pool infrastructure (dual pools: tether + airgap)
184	  readonly RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_TETHER="-tether"
185	  readonly RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_AIRGAP="-airgap"
186	  readonly RBGC_WORKER_POOL_SUFFIX="-pool"
187	  readonly RBGC_PATH_WORKER_POOLS="/workerPools"
188	
189	  readonly ZRBGC_KINDLED=1
190	}
191	
192	zrbgc_sentinel() {
193	  test "${ZRBGC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgc not kindled - call zrbgc_kindle first"
194	}
195	
196	# eof
197	
198

# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle GCP Constants - Implementation (no printf required)

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
# (Module state remains ZRBGC_* per BCG; external constants use RBGC_*)
test -z "${ZRBGC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBGC_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbgc_*)

zrbgc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBGC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc already kindled"

  # Global Resource Naming (Google Cloud global namespace)
  # These resources compete in globally-unique namespaces across all of GCP
  # Pattern: {prefix}-{type}-{name}-{timestamp} where timestamp is YYMMDDHHMMSS
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX="rbwg"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR="p"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT="d"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_BUCKET="b"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_FORMAT="+%y%m%d%H%M%S"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN=12
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX="[0-9]{${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN}}"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_NAME_MAX=10

  # Global resource validation patterns
  # Payor:  rbwg-p-YYMMDDHHMMSS
  # Depot:  rbwg-d-[name]-YYMMDDHHMMSS
  # Bucket: rbwg-b-[name]-YYMMDDHHMMSS
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PAYOR_REGEX="^${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX}$"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_REGEX="^${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-[a-z0-9-]+-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX}$"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_BUCKET_REGEX="^${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_BUCKET}-[a-z0-9-]+-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX}$"

  # Basic Configuration
  readonly RBGC_ADMIN_ROLE="rbw-admin"
  readonly RBGC_PAYOR_ROLE="rbw-payor"
  readonly RBGC_PAYOR_APP_NAME="Recipe Bottle Payor"

  # Service Account Prefixes
  readonly RBGC_MASON_PREFIX="mason"

  # Timeouts
  readonly RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC=90
  readonly RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC=3
  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX=7
  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC=10

  # URL Roots & Well-known Endpoints
  readonly RBGC_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL="https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM="https://iam.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM="https://cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_SERVICEUSAGE="https://serviceusage.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY="https://artifactregistry.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD="https://cloudbuild.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING="https://cloudbilling.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE="https://storage.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_SECRETMANAGER="https://secretmanager.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_CONSOLE_URL="https://console.cloud.google.com/"
  readonly RBGC_SIGNUP_URL="https://cloud.google.com/free"

  # OAuth Scopes
  readonly RBGC_SCOPE_CLOUD_PLATFORM="https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform"

  # Service Usage Service Identifiers
  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_IAM="iam.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_CRM="cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY="artifactregistry.googleapis.com"

  # Email/Domain Assembly
  readonly RBGC_SA_EMAIL_DOMAIN="iam.gserviceaccount.com"

  # API Version Paths
  readonly RBGC_IAM_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_CRM_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_CRM_V3="/v3"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1BETA1="/v1beta1"
  readonly RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_CLOUDBILLING_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1="/storage/v1"
  readonly RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_UPLOAD="/upload/storage/v1"
  readonly RBGC_SECRETMANAGER_V1="/v1"

  # REST Path Fragments
  readonly RBGC_PATH_PROJECTS="/projects"
  readonly RBGC_PATH_LOCATIONS="/locations"
  readonly RBGC_PATH_REPOSITORIES="/repositories"
  readonly RBGC_PATH_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS="/serviceAccounts"
  readonly RBGC_PATH_KEYS="/keys"

  # REST Operation Suffixes
  readonly RBGC_CRM_GET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX=":getIamPolicy"
  readonly RBGC_CRM_SET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX=":setIamPolicy"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_ENABLE_SUFFIX=":enable"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_PATH_SERVICES="/services"

  # Operation Prefixes
  readonly RBGC_OP_PREFIX_GLOBAL="operations/"

  # Ark Artifact Basenames (₢A_AAK layout)
  # Each ark type is a plain basename sibling under <prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/.
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE="image"
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT="about"
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH="vouch"
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_DIAGS="diags"
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ATTEST="attest"
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_POUCH="pouch"

  # GAR Categorical Namespaces (₢A_AAK layout)
  # Top-level prefix-rooted namespaces under which hallmark/reliquary/enshrine
  # arks are stored as plain basename siblings. Consumed by rbgl_GarLayout.sh.
  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS="hallmarks"
  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES="reliquaries"
  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_ENSHRINES="enshrines"

  # Reliquary Tool Basenames (₢A_AAK layout)
  # Canonical tool names under <prefix>reliquaries/<date>/. Authoritative
  # manifest lives in rbgji/rbgji01-inscribe-mirror.sh.
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD="gcloud"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER="docker"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE="alpine"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT="syft"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT="binfmt"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO="skopeo"

  # Fact-file filenames (written to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR by producers, read by tests)
  readonly RBF_FACT_HALLMARK="rbf_fact_hallmark"
  readonly RBF_FACT_BUILD_ID="rbf_fact_build_id"
  readonly RBF_FACT_GAR_ROOT="rbf_fact_gar_root"
  readonly RBF_FACT_ARK_STEM="rbf_fact_ark_stem"
  readonly RBF_FACT_ARK_YIELD="rbf_fact_ark_yield"
  readonly RBF_FACT_RELIQUARY="rbf_fact_reliquary"

  # Payor fact-file filenames (depot/governor identifying values)
  readonly RBGP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID="rbgp_fact_depot_project_id"
  readonly RBGP_FACT_GOVERNOR_SA_EMAIL="rbgp_fact_governor_sa_email"

  # Artifact Registry (GAR) Composition
  readonly RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX="-docker.pkg.dev"


  # Canonical Role IDs
  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_READER="roles/artifactregistry.reader"
  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_WRITER="roles/artifactregistry.writer"
  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_ADMIN="roles/artifactregistry.admin"
  readonly RBGC_ROLE_CONTAINERANALYSIS_OCCURRENCES_VIEWER="roles/containeranalysis.occurrences.viewer"
  readonly RBGC_ROLE_CLOUDBUILD_BUILDS_EDITOR="roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor"

  # Common API Base Paths (project-independent)
  readonly RBGC_API_BASE_GCS="${RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE}${RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1}"

  # Cloud Resource Manager - Liens API
  readonly RBGC_API_CRM_LIST_LIENS="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens"
  readonly RBGC_API_CRM_DELETE_LIEN="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens"

  # Google Cloud Storage (GCS) APIs (project-independent)
  readonly RBGC_API_GCS_BUCKETS="${RBGC_API_BASE_GCS}/b"

  readonly RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM="linux/amd64"


  # Worker pool infrastructure (dual pools: tether + airgap)
  readonly RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_TETHER="-tether"
  readonly RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_AIRGAP="-airgap"
  readonly RBGC_WORKER_POOL_SUFFIX="-pool"
  readonly RBGC_PATH_WORKER_POOLS="/workerPools"

  readonly ZRBGC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbgc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBGC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgc not kindled - call zrbgc_kindle first"
}

# eof

568	rbgp_depot_levy() {
569	  zrbgp_sentinel
570	
571	  local -r z_depot_name="${1:-}"
572	  local -r z_region="${RBRR_GCP_REGION}"
573	
574	  buc_doc_brief "Create new depot infrastructure following RBAGS specification"
575	  buc_doc_param "depot_name" "Depot name (lowercase/numbers/hyphens, max ${RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_NAME_MAX} chars)"
576	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
577	
578	  buc_step 'Validate input parameters'
579	  test -n "${z_depot_name}" || buc_die "Depot name required as first argument"
580	  
581	  if ! [[ "${z_depot_name}" =~ ^[a-z0-9-]+$ ]]; then
582	    buc_die "Depot name must contain only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens"
583	  fi
584	  
585	  if test "${#z_depot_name}" -gt "${RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_NAME_MAX}"; then
586	    buc_die "Depot name must be ${RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_NAME_MAX} characters or less"
587	  fi
588	
589	  # Validate region exists in Artifact Registry locations
590	  buc_log_args 'Validating region exists in Artifact Registry locations'
591	  local z_token
592	  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate as Payor for region validation"
593	
594	  local -r z_locations_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/projects/${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID}/locations"
595	  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_locations_url}" "${z_token}" "region_validation"
596	  rbgu_http_require_ok "Validate region" "region_validation"
597	  
598	  local z_valid_regions
599	  z_valid_regions=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "region_validation" '.locations[].locationId' | tr '\n' ' ') || buc_die "Failed to parse region list"
600	  
601	  if ! [[ " ${z_valid_regions} " =~ [[:space:]]${z_region}[[:space:]] ]]; then
602	    buc_die "Invalid region. Valid regions: ${z_valid_regions}"
603	  fi
604	
605	  buc_step 'Authenticate as Payor'
606	  test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID is not set"
607	  test -n "${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID is not set"
608	  test -n "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID is not set"
609	  
610	  local z_token
611	  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate as Payor via OAuth"
612	
613	  buc_step 'Generate depot project ID'
614	  local z_timestamp
615	  date "${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_FORMAT}" > "${ZRBGP_SCRATCH_FILE}" \
616	    || buc_die "Failed to generate timestamp"
617	  z_timestamp=$(<"${ZRBGP_SCRATCH_FILE}")
618	  local -r z_depot_project_id="${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-${z_depot_name}-${z_timestamp}"
619	  
620	  if test "${#z_depot_project_id}" -gt 30; then
621	    buc_die "Generated project ID too long (${#z_depot_project_id} > 30): ${z_depot_project_id}"
622	  fi
623	  
624	  buc_log_args "Generated depot project ID: ${z_depot_project_id}"
625	  buf_write_fact "${RBGP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" "${z_depot_project_id}"
626	
627	  buc_step 'Create depot project'
628	  local -r z_create_project_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_create_project.json"
629	
630	  # OAuth users create projects without parent (per MPCR)
631	  jq -n \
632	    --arg projectId "${z_depot_project_id}" \
633	    --arg displayName "RB Depot ${z_depot_name}" \
634	    '{
635	      projectId: $projectId,
636	      displayName: $displayName
637	    }' > "${z_create_project_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build project creation body"
638	
639	  local -r z_create_project_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V3}/projects"
640	  rbgu_http_json_lro_ok \
641	    "Create depot project" \
642	    "${z_token}" \
643	    "${z_create_project_url}" \
644	    "depot_project_create" \
645	    "${z_create_project_body}" \
646	    ".name" \
647	    "${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}" \
648	    "operations/" \
649	    "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" \
650	    "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
651	
652	  buc_step 'Link billing account'
653	  local -r z_billing_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_billing_link.json"
654	  jq -n \
655	    --arg billingAccountName "billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}" \
656	    '{
657	      billingAccountName: $billingAccountName
658	    }' > "${z_billing_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build billing link body"
659	
660	  local -r z_billing_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING}${RBGC_CLOUDBILLING_V1}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}/billingInfo"
661	  rbgu_http_json "PUT" "${z_billing_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_billing_link" "${z_billing_body}"
662	  rbgu_http_require_ok "Link billing account" "depot_billing_link"
663	
664	  buc_step 'Get depot project number'
665	  local -r z_project_info_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V3}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}"
666	  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_project_info_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_project_info"
667	  rbgu_http_require_ok "Get project info" "depot_project_info"
668	  
669	  local z_project_number
670	  # CRM v3 returns project number in name field as "projects/{number}"
671	  z_project_number=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_project_info" '.name | sub("projects/"; "")') || buc_die "Failed to get project number"
672	  test -n "${z_project_number}" || buc_die "Project number is empty"
673	
674	  buc_step 'Enable depot project APIs'
675	  local -r z_api_services="artifactregistry cloudbuild cloudresourcemanager containeranalysis iam serviceusage storage"
676	  for z_service in ${z_api_services}; do
677	    rbgu_api_enable "${z_service}" "${z_depot_project_id}" "${z_token}"
678	  done
679	
680	  buc_step 'Create dual worker pools (tether + airgap)'
681	  local -r z_pool_stem="rbw-${z_depot_name}${RBGC_WORKER_POOL_SUFFIX}"
682	  local -r z_pool_parent="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD}${RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}/locations/${z_region}${RBGC_PATH_WORKER_POOLS}"
683	
684	  # Tether pool (default egress — public internet access)
685	  local -r z_tether_id="${z_pool_stem}${RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_TETHER}"
686	  local -r z_tether_create_url="${z_pool_parent}?workerPoolId=${z_tether_id}"
687	  local -r z_tether_create_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_pool_tether_create.json"
688	
689	  jq -n --arg machineType "${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE}" \
690	    '{
691	      privatePoolV1Config: {
692	        workerConfig: {
693	          machineType: $machineType
694	        }
695	      }
696	    }' > "${z_tether_create_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build tether pool creation body"
697	
698	  rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_tether_create_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_pool_tether_create" "${z_tether_create_body}"
699	  local z_tether_create_code
700	  z_tether_create_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "depot_pool_tether_create") || buc_die "Bad tether pool creation HTTP code"
701	  case "${z_tether_create_code}" in
702	    200|201) buc_log_args "Tether pool ${z_tether_id} created" ;;
703	    409)     buc_log_args "Tether pool ${z_tether_id} already exists (idempotent)" ;;
704	    *)       buc_die "Failed to create tether pool: HTTP ${z_tether_create_code}" ;;
705	  esac
706	
707	  # Airgap pool (NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS — no public internet)
708	  local -r z_airgap_id="${z_pool_stem}${RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_AIRGAP}"
709	  local -r z_airgap_create_url="${z_pool_parent}?workerPoolId=${z_airgap_id}"
710	  local -r z_airgap_create_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_pool_airgap_create.json"
711	
712	  jq -n --arg machineType "${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE}" \
713	    '{
714	      privatePoolV1Config: {
715	        workerConfig: {
716	          machineType: $machineType
717	        },
718	        networkConfig: {
719	          egressOption: "NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS"
720	        }
721	      }
722	    }' > "${z_airgap_create_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build airgap pool creation body"
723	
724	  rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_airgap_create_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_pool_airgap_create" "${z_airgap_create_body}"
725	  local z_airgap_create_code
726	  z_airgap_create_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "depot_pool_airgap_create") || buc_die "Bad airgap pool creation HTTP code"
727	  case "${z_airgap_create_code}" in
728	    200|201) buc_log_args "Airgap pool ${z_airgap_id} created" ;;
729	    409)     buc_log_args "Airgap pool ${z_airgap_id} already exists (idempotent)" ;;
730	    *)       buc_die "Failed to create airgap pool: HTTP ${z_airgap_create_code}" ;;
731	  esac
732	
733	  local -r z_pool_resource="projects/${z_depot_project_id}/locations/${z_region}/workerPools/${z_pool_stem}"
734	  buc_log_args "Pool stem: ${z_pool_resource}"
735	
736	  # Note: OAuth Payor doesn't need explicit permissions on depot since it uses user identity
737	  # Skip Payor permission grants - OAuth user context provides necessary access
738	
739	  buc_step 'Verify IAM propagation before resource creation'
740	  local -r z_preflight_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}/locations/${z_region}/repositories"
741	  rbgu_poll_until_ok "AR IAM propagation" "${z_preflight_url}" "${z_token}" "iam_preflight"
742	
743	  buc_step 'Create build bucket'
744	  local -r z_build_bucket="${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_BUCKET}-${z_depot_name}-${z_timestamp}"
745	  local -r z_bucket_req="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_bucket_create_req.json"
746	  jq -n \
747	    --arg name "${z_build_bucket}" \
748	    --arg location "${z_region}" \
749	    --arg project "${z_depot_project_id}" \
750	    '{
751	      name: $name,
752	      location: $location,
753	      storageClass: "STANDARD",
754	      lifecycle: { rule: [ { action: { type: "Delete" }, condition: { age: 1 } } ] }
755	    }' > "${z_bucket_req}" || buc_die "Failed to create bucket request JSON"
756	
757	  local -r z_bucket_create_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE}${RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1}/b?project=${z_depot_project_id}"
758	  rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_bucket_create_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_bucket_create" "${z_bucket_req}"
759	  
760	  local z_bucket_code
761	  z_bucket_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "depot_bucket_create") || buc_die "Bad bucket creation HTTP code"
762	  case "${z_bucket_code}" in
763	    200|201) buc_log_args "Build bucket ${z_build_bucket} created" ;;
764	    409)     buc_die "Build bucket ${z_build_bucket} already exists" ;;
765	    *)       buc_die "Failed to create build bucket: HTTP ${z_bucket_code}" ;;
766	  esac
767	
768	  buc_step 'Create container repository'
769	  local -r z_repository_name="rbw-${z_depot_name}-repository"
770	  local -r z_parent="projects/${z_depot_project_id}/locations/${z_region}"
771	  local -r z_create_repo_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_parent}/repositories?repositoryId=${z_repository_name}"
772	  local -r z_create_repo_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_create_repo.json"
773	  
774	  jq -n '{format:"DOCKER"}' > "${z_create_repo_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build create-repo body"
775	
776	  rbgu_http_json_lro_ok \
777	    "Create container repository" \
778	    "${z_token}" \
779	    "${z_create_repo_url}" \
780	    "depot_repo_create" \
781	    "${z_create_repo_body}" \
782	    ".name" \
783	    "${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}" \
784	    "${RBGC_OP_PREFIX_GLOBAL}" \
785	    "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" \
786	    "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
787	
788	  buc_step 'Verify IAM API is ready for service account creation'
789	  local -r z_iam_preflight_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}/serviceAccounts"
790	  rbgu_poll_until_ok "IAM API" "${z_iam_preflight_url}" "${z_token}" "iam_sa_preflight"
791	
792	  buc_step 'Create Mason service account'
793	  local -r z_mason_name="${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-${z_depot_name}"
794	  local -r z_mason_display_name="Mason for RB Depot: ${z_depot_name}"
795	  local -r z_create_sa_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_create_mason.json"
796	  
797	  jq -n \
798	    --arg accountId "${z_mason_name}" \
799	    --arg displayName "${z_mason_display_name}" \
800	    '{
801	      accountId: $accountId,
802	      serviceAccount: {
803	        displayName: $displayName
804	      }
805	    }' > "${z_create_sa_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build Mason creation body"
806	
807	  local -r z_create_sa_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}/serviceAccounts"
808	  rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_create_sa_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_mason_create" "${z_create_sa_body}"
809	  rbgu_http_require_ok "Create Mason service account" "depot_mason_create"
810	  
811	  local z_mason_sa_email
812	  z_mason_sa_email=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_mason_create" '.email') || buc_die "Failed to get Mason email"
813	
814	  buc_step 'Verify Mason service account propagation'
815	  local -r z_mason_sa_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}/serviceAccounts/${z_mason_sa_email}"
816	  rbgu_poll_until_ok "Mason SA" "${z_mason_sa_url}" "${z_token}" "mason_sa_preflight"
817	
818	  buc_step 'Configure Mason permissions'
819	  # Repository admin (AR repo IAM requires email, not numeric ID)
820	  rbgi_add_repo_iam_role "${z_token}" "${z_depot_project_id}" "${z_mason_sa_email}" "${z_region}" "${z_repository_name}" \
821	    "roles/artifactregistry.writer"
822	
823	  # Bucket viewer (GCS bucket IAM requires email, not numeric ID)
824	  rbgi_add_bucket_iam_role "${z_token}" "${z_build_bucket}" "${z_mason_sa_email}" "roles/storage.objectViewer"
825	
826	  # Project viewer
827	  rbgi_add_project_iam_role "${z_token}" "Grant Mason Project Viewer" "projects/${z_depot_project_id}" \
828	    "roles/viewer" "serviceAccount:${z_mason_sa_email}" "mason-viewer"
829	
830	  # Logs writer (for Cloud Build logs to Cloud Logging)
831	  rbgi_add_project_iam_role "${z_token}" "Grant Mason Logs Writer" "projects/${z_depot_project_id}" \
832	    "roles/logging.logWriter" "serviceAccount:${z_mason_sa_email}" "mason-logs-writer"
833	
834	  buc_step 'Provision Cloud Build service agent'
835	  # The Cloud Build Service Agent (service-{PN}@gcp-sa-cloudbuild.iam.gserviceaccount.com)
836	  # needs serviceAccountTokenCreator on Mason to impersonate it during builds.create.
837	  # The service agent is auto-created when the Cloud Build API is enabled; its email is
838	  # deterministic from the project number. See RBSCIP for the two-SA distinction.
839	  rbgu_provision_service_agent "cloudbuild" "${z_depot_project_id}" "${z_token}" > /dev/null \
840	    || buc_die "Failed to provision Cloud Build service agent"
841	  local -r z_cb_service_agent="service-${z_project_number}@gcp-sa-cloudbuild.${RBGC_SA_EMAIL_DOMAIN}"
842	  buc_log_args "CB service agent: ${z_cb_service_agent}"
843	
844	  buc_step 'Enable Cloud Build service agent to impersonate Mason'
845	  rbgi_add_sa_iam_role "${z_token}" "${z_mason_sa_email}" "${z_cb_service_agent}" "roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator"
846	
847	  buc_step 'Update depot tracking'
848	  zrbgp_depot_list_update || buc_die "Failed to update depot tracking after creation"
849	
850	  buc_step 'Display depot configuration'
851	  buc_success 'Depot creation successful'
852	  buc_info "Mason service account: ${z_mason_sa_email}"
853	  buc_info "Update RBRR configuration:"
854	  buc_bare "  RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID=${z_depot_project_id}"
855	  buc_bare "  RBRR_GCP_REGION=${z_region}"
856	  buc_bare "  RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY=${z_repository_name}"
857	  buc_bare "  RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM=${z_pool_stem}"
858	  buc_info "Next: mantle Governor for this depot:"
859	  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR}"
860	}
861
