384	/// Set `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` in rbrr.env to `project_id` and commit. Used
385	/// by case 3 to satisfy `rbgp_governor_mantle`'s precondition that the
386	/// depot project is named in the regime. The commit is separate from the
387	/// throwaway-prefix commit so the audit trail shows the levy step that
388	/// produced the value.
389	fn rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id(root: &Path, project_id: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
390	    let rbrr = root.join(RBTDRP_RBRR_FILE);
391	    let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&rbrr)
392	        .map_err(|e| format!("rbtdrp: read {}: {}", rbrr.display(), e))?;
393	    let new_content = rbtdrp_replace_env_fields(
394	        &content,
395	        &[(RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID, project_id)],
396	    );
397	    std::fs::write(&rbrr, &new_content)
398	        .map_err(|e| format!("rbtdrp: write {}: {}", rbrr.display(), e))?;
399	
400	    let commit_msg = format!(
401	        "pristine-lifecycle fixture: set {}={}",
402	        RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID, project_id
403	    );
404	    rbtdrp_git_add_and_commit(root, RBTDRP_RBRR_FILE, &commit_msg)
405	}

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle GCP Payor - Billing and Destructive Operations CLI

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

zrbgp_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  local z_command="${1:-}"

  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
  source "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgo_OAuth.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgu_Utility.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgi_IAM.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrp_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgp_Payor.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"

  buc_log_args 'Initialize modules'
  zbuv_kindle
  zburd_kindle
  zrbcc_kindle

  zrbrr_kindle
  # Differential furnish: depot_levy establishes the depot — skip enforce
  # which requires RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID and other fields it will populate.
  case "${z_command}" in
    rbgp_depot_levy) ;;
    *)                 zrbrr_enforce ;;
  esac
  zrbdc_kindle

  zrbgc_kindle

  source "${RBBC_rbrp_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRP: ${RBBC_rbrp_file}"
  zrbrp_kindle
  zrbrp_enforce

  zrbgo_kindle
  zrbgu_kindle
  zrbgi_kindle
  zrbgp_kindle

  zbuz_kindle
  zrbz_kindle
}

buc_execute rbgp_ "Recipe Bottle Payor" zrbgp_furnish "$@"

# eof

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Derived Constants - Credential file path resolution

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBDC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbdc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBDC_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbdc_*)

zrbdc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBDC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbdc already kindled"
  zrbrr_sentinel

  # Ensure secrets directory and role subdirectories exist
  mkdir -p "${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_governor}" \
           "${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_retriever}" \
           "${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_director}" \
           "${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_payor}" \
           "${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_assay}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to create secrets directories under: ${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}"

  # One-shot migration: move old flat rbra-{role}.env to {role}/rbra.env
  local z_mig_role=""
  for z_mig_role in "${RBCC_role_governor}" "${RBCC_role_retriever}" "${RBCC_role_director}"; do
    local z_mig_old="${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/rbra-${z_mig_role}.env"
    local z_mig_new="${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${z_mig_role}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"
    if test -f "${z_mig_old}" && test -f "${z_mig_new}"; then
      buc_die "Migration ambiguous for role ${z_mig_role}: both ${z_mig_old} and ${z_mig_new} exist; delete the obsolete file before continuing"
    fi
    if test -f "${z_mig_old}" && ! test -f "${z_mig_new}"; then
      mv "${z_mig_old}" "${z_mig_new}" || buc_die "Failed to migrate: ${z_mig_old} → ${z_mig_new}"
      local z_has_role=0
      local z_mig_line
      while IFS= read -r z_mig_line; do
        case "${z_mig_line}" in RBRA_ROLE=*) z_has_role=1; break ;; esac
      done < "${z_mig_new}"
      if test "${z_has_role}" = "0"; then
        printf 'RBRA_ROLE=%s\n' "${z_mig_role}" >> "${z_mig_new}"
      fi
    fi
  done

  # Payor migration: rbro.env -> payor/rbro.env
  local z_mig_payor_old="${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_rbro_file}"
  local z_mig_payor_new="${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_payor}/${RBCC_rbro_file}"
  if test -f "${z_mig_payor_old}" && test -f "${z_mig_payor_new}"; then
    buc_die "Migration ambiguous: both ${z_mig_payor_old} and ${z_mig_payor_new} exist; delete the obsolete file before continuing"
  fi
  if test -f "${z_mig_payor_old}"; then
    mv "${z_mig_payor_old}" "${z_mig_payor_new}" || buc_die "Failed to migrate: ${z_mig_payor_old} → ${z_mig_payor_new}"
  fi

  # Derive credential file paths from RBRR_SECRETS_DIR
  readonly RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE="${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_governor}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"
  readonly RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE="${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_retriever}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"
  readonly RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE="${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"
  readonly RBDC_PAYOR_RBRO_FILE="${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_payor}/${RBCC_rbro_file}"
  readonly RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE="${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_assay}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"

  # Derive depot identity from (RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER).
  # Project ID, GAR repository, pool stem, and bucket fall out at kindle.
  readonly RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID="${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}"
  readonly RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY="${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}-gar"
  readonly RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM="${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}${RBGC_WORKER_POOL_SUFFIX}"
  readonly RBDC_GCS_BUCKET="${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_BUCKET}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}"

  # Derive full pool resource paths from stem (suffixes match RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_TETHER/AIRGAP)
  readonly RBDC_POOL_TETHER="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBRR_GCP_REGION}/workerPools/${RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM}${RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_TETHER}"
  readonly RBDC_POOL_AIRGAP="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBRR_GCP_REGION}/workerPools/${RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM}${RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_AIRGAP}"

  readonly ZRBDC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbdc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBDC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbdc not kindled - call zrbdc_kindle first"
}

# eof

lines: # Project ID, GAR repository, pool stem, and bucket fall out at kindle.
+  # Type markers and suffixes are literals here (rather than RBGC consts)
+  # to keep rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering.
# Derive full pool resource paths from stem (suffixes match RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_TETHER/AIRGAP)

RBRR drops RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID, RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY, RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM and adds RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER (format ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$, length 1-26). RBDC adds RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID (${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}), RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY (${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar), RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM (${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool), and RBDC_GCS_BUCKET (${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}); type markers and pool/tether/airgap suffixes are literal strings inside RBDC to keep rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering (existing furnishers run rbdc-then-rbgc in most modules, rbgc-then-rbdc in others — uniformity left for a future cleanup). RBGC drops obsolete RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_REGEX and RBGC_GLOBAL_BUCKET_REGEX (project_id and bucket are now installation-prefix-anchored, not timestamp-suffixed) and gains depot-state vocabulary tinder constants (RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE/BROKEN/DELETE_REQUESTED) plus RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT for the per-moniker fact-file shape coming in Step 4. RBGD switches RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID -> RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID throughout, drops the prefix-strip-and-timestamp depot-name extraction logic (RBGD_DEPOT_NAME is now just RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER), and reshapes RBGD_GCS_BUCKET via RBDC_GCS_BUCKET. Marshal-zero (rblm_cli.sh) banner copy and sed cases adjusted to the new field set. Compose bridge: rbob_bottle.sh writes a temp env file (rbob_rbdc_compose.env) at zrbob_kindle with RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID and RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY, added to the docker compose --env-file list; .rbk/rbob_compose.yml image refs switched from RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID/RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY to the RBDC equivalents. Operator's .rbk/rbrr.env reshaped to the new field set with RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER=depot10041 placeholder. Mechanical RBRR_X -> RBDC_X sweep across all consumers: rbgg_Governor, rbgv_AccessProbe, rbgp_Payor (depot-list regex sites replaced with string-prefix match against ${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-, post-levy 'Update RBRR configuration' banner replaced with informational 'Depot resources created' summary, governor-mantle docstring and error message rewritten around the new derivation), rbgp_cli (differential-furnish comment annotated Step-2-pending), rbgb_Buckets, rbga_ArtifactRegistry, rbob_bottle (GAR ref via RBDC), rbrn_cli, rbfv_FoundryVerify, rbfc_FoundryCore, rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild, rbfl_FoundryLedger, rbhpq_quota_build (also RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM -> RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM), rbhpb_base. Onboarding probes that file-scan rbrr.env to detect 'depot configured' switched from RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID -> RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER: rbhocc_crash_course, rbhodf_director_first_build, rbhoda_director_airgap, rbhodb_director_bind, rbhodg_director_graft. Theurge minimal-touch: rbtdrp_pristine.rs RBTDRP_RBRR_BLANK_FIELDS shrinks to {CLOUD_PREFIX, RUNTIME_PREFIX, DEPOT_MONIKER}; helper rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id renamed to rbtdrp_install_depot_moniker (case 3 call site updated, but the ordering is service-tier-broken until Step 5 reshapes case 3). rbtdrf_fast.rs negative tests rbrr-missing-project-id -> rbrr-missing-moniker (unset RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER) and rbrr-bad-pool-stem -> rbrr-bad-moniker (export RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER=BAD-MONIKER triggers format check). Build green: rbtd-b, rbtd-t (58/58 unit tests), rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast (97/97 across enrollment-validation/regime-validation/regime-smoke/handbook-render), vow-b. Step 2 (rbgp_depot_levy reshape — drop timestamp, drop CLI arg, use RBDC, drop post-levy banner) is the next pace; rbgp_depot_levy in this state is left functionally intact with its old CLI-arg flow but its post-creation values can no longer be 'pasted into RBRR' (they're derived now), so the function is effectively unusable until Step 2 lands.

content.text:
Size guard tripped — `jjx_record` blocked at **62608 bytes** vs **50000 byte limit** (12608 over).

**Breakdown:**
```
        8363 bytes  Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh
        6143 bytes  Tools/rbk/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh
        5100 bytes  Tools/rbk/rbgg_Governor.sh
        4137 bytes  Tools/rbk/rbgd_DepotConstants.sh
        4027 bytes  Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrp_pristine.rs
        3580 bytes  Tools/rbk/rbga_ArtifactRegistry.sh
        3542 bytes  Tools/rbk/rbh0/rbhpq_quota_build.sh
        2553 bytes  Tools/rbk/rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh
        1952 bytes  Tools/rbk/rbrr_regime.sh
        1900 bytes  Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh
  ... and 18 more files (~21k bytes)
```

**My read on the trade-off:** This change is genuinely atomic. Each sub-piece taken alone breaks the build:
- Kindle infrastructure (rbrr/rbdc/rbgc/rbgd) alone → consumers reference undefined fields
- Sweep alone (RBRR_X → RBDC_X in consumers) → RBDC_* doesn't exist yet
- Marshal-zero / compose bridge / probes — each ties to the kindle field set

A clean split would need a temporary compat shim (export RBRR_X aliases to RBDC_X values during transition) which adds throwaway code we'd immediately tear out, plus two notches plus rework. Net cost likely exceeds the value of staying under 50k.

**Recommend: raise the limit for this single notch.** The size is high because the sweep is wide (~25 files), not because individual files are bloated. The biggest individual file (rbgp_Payor.sh at 8363 bytes) reflects the RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID swap across many API call sites + the post-levy banner softening + the two regex-to-string-prefix conversions — all mechanically necessary.

What would you like — **raise** (and to what — e.g., 70000?) **or split**?

content.text:
You're right — sloppy wording on my part. Step 2 is a **step (notch) within the current pace ₢BBAAM**, not a separate pace.

The docket scope for ₢BBAAM lists six items: depot_levy reshape, RBRR field collapse, depot_list reshape, pristine-fixture autodetect retrofit, marshal-zero adjustment, and RBS0/banner updates. Step 1 covered the RBRR field collapse, marshal-zero adjustment, and the kindle infrastructure that makes the rest possible. Steps 2-7 each address remaining docket items as further notches under the same coronet. ₢BBAAM closes (wraps) when all six docket items are landed.

So the question isn't "draft the next pace" — it's "draft Step 2 of this pace." Want me to proceed with that?

message.content: give me a prompt to start that process in a clean chat because we are over context for this one.

content.text:
Here's a self-contained starter prompt:

---

Mount ₣BB and resume work on ₢BBAAM (rbrr-depot-identity-collapse). Step 1 landed at commit 3c9d38fb (kindle infrastructure + mechanical sweep across ~28 files; build/fast-suite 97/97 green).

**Before proceeding to Step 2, curry the BBAAM-locked design decisions into the paddock** so they persist for the rest of the pace. Decisions made in conversation, not yet in the paddock:

- **Maximal collapse, not minimal.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`.
- **CLOUD_PREFIX flows uniformly** through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-specific). Payor stays on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated, out of scope).
- **Naming shapes:** project `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`; GAR `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`); pool `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`; bucket `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`. Type markers (`d-`, `b-`) and pool suffixes are literals inside RBDC to keep rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering.
- **409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409s left as today (bucket fatal; AR/pools idempotent).
- **Mason SA name unchanged** (`mason-${moniker}`); design call deferred.
- **Theurge-side moniker autodetect** for pristine fixture cases 2-3: family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the 100000 floor live in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not in payor code. Six-digit numeric increment.
- **Fact-file shape:** `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension `depot`); content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE/BROKEN/DELETE_REQUESTED` (tinder constants in `rbgc_Constants.sh`, already added in Step 1).

**Then propose Step 2 (`rbgp_depot_levy` reshape) for real-time review before any code change.** Step 2's intent per the docket: drop the timestamp; drop the CLI `<depot-name>` positional arg in favor of `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` from kindled regime; use `RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM`, `RBDC_GCS_BUCKET` instead of inline computation; remove the post-levy banner (already softened to "Depot resources created" in Step 1, but under maximal collapse the operator needs no post-levy paste at all — just confirmation). Also remove the differential-furnish skip in `rbgp_cli.sh` (annotated "Step-2-pending" in Step 1) so levy goes through `zrbrr_enforce` like every other depot operation.

**Steps 3-7 remain in scope under ₢BBAAM** (not separate paces): Step 3 unmake reshape, Step 4 depot_list per-moniker fact-file emission, Step 5 pristine-fixture autodetect retrofit, Step 6 fast-suite test sweep audit, Step 7 RBS0 + RBSDE banner copy. Pace wraps when all six docket items land.

Honor the paddock's Release-branch execution contract and the "no pre-engineered choices beyond intent" / "real-time review at each step before commit" discipline. Each step ends with build green (`tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh`, `tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh`, `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh`) + a notch.

---

Use that as the first message of the new chat. The new agent will run `jjx_open` + `jjx_orient BB`, read the gazette, and pick up from here.
